reviews.review_msg: really good professor, set course well for student lack stat experiences  I am from stat minor

Prof Estep is a very caring professor. He pays very close attention to how his students are doing in his class and always adjusts the content and pace of his lectures to better accommodate his students. He gave 0 exams and instead the grades were all from projects - which he thoroughly helps his students to complete.
Wonderful individual as a teacher. The best I ever met!
Probably one of the more useful courses you will take in your time at SFU. That being said, it is tough and requires studying. Exams were hard but doable given 6 cheatsheets.

Richard is a very capable and genuinely caring professor. He is approachable, kind, and always willing to help, don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions or concerns. BUT his lectures are not effective at all. Slides contain mistakes, very few written examples. Self study is the key to pass the course

it was the simplest grading scheme for a 4th year stats course, 30 for assignments 30 for midterm 40 for final. the exams are definitely on the hard side but he gave 6 double sided cheatsheets for the final which was fair of him. the course is interesting and different from the usual data analysis stat courses. 
Cool guy, will recommend.

This course itself is extremely hard and he teaches THE WORST. I'd rather go to his lectures if I am having trouble sleeping. The midterm was no where near to what was taught in class and I mean that. The class average was 48% which clearly shows people were not ready for the absurd questions he put. The assignments don't teach anything. STAY AWAY

Rambles so much, and his handwriting is so bad that I don't want to look at it. I read the textbook instead because I couldn't rely on his teaching style. Midterm average was below 50% which means somethins wrong in this class. Would never take a class with him.
The prof just rambles on in class. Doesnt know how to actually teach clearly. His midterm was beyond the notes and assignments that the clss average was 48% only. HE DOESNT TEST THE WAY HE TEACHES.

Assignments marked too generously so barely any feedback, lectures are disorganized and he rambles. Midterm difficulty was beyond assignments and practice midterm. Midterm was missing a graph and questions lacked clarity. You're better off taking the class another sem with a diff prof if you're able to

Gary Parker is a responsible and nice professor. Time series is a very difficult concept in the statistics industry, but he explained it very well. There are mathematical questions in the course that are tough but very helpful for us to understand the concept. Gary sacrifices his precious time for those students who are struggling with the course.
Lots of theories and calculations, but most of the time you don't understand them. Even though I got a B, I didn't really learn anything

Extremely boring lectures, extremely boring. He spends so much time explaining theoretical materials which is practically useless and most students, like me, forget everything he teaches the day after his final exam. Let's be real, who is going to calculate auto-correlation manually with a pen and paper when everything is done by computer nowadays

Gary's class covers diverse topics, often lacking continuity. Quizzes and midterms are strict, with stringent process requirements and little credit for partial work. Both teaching and grading need refinement. Attendance and proactive homework are crucial for success here."
Hard test. The scoring criteria are extremely unfair. Obvious errors in his notes. The tutorials are useless.
